reasoning: 17 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 is an incredibly difficult feat. The last album to spend even close to that time consecutively was Taylor Swift's massive release The Tortured Poets Department, which achieved 12 consecutive weeks, and Morgan Wallen's One Thing at a Time, which also achieved 12. Prior to the last few years, the record for most consecutive weeks in the modern SoundScan era was 20 weeks by The Bodyguard in 1993, well over 30 years ago. An album spending 17 consecutive weeks at #1 requires both phenomenal, sustained popular reception and a complete lack of commercially strong competitive album drops across roughly four months, which is extremely rare in the current streaming-driven and fast-moving music release cycle. Because the base rate for this event across all music releases is well under 1%, the probability is assessed as 0.02 (the floor allowed).
